title: Morroccan Chili Crisp
description: A savory and umami Moroccan chili crisp recipe featuring a blend of spices and tandoor-grilled flavors for a low-fat-friendly brunch dish.

ingredients.name: 8 ounces chicken breast, sliced into thin strips
Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water and drain well. In a medium saucepan, bring water or broth to a boil. Add quinoa, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes or until all the liquid is absorbed.

While the quinoa cooks, in a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add chicken strips and sauté for about 4-5 minutes on each side or until browned and cooked through. Remove from pan and set aside.

In the same skillet, add remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Sauté onion and garlic for about 3 minutes until softened. Stir in cumin, paprika, coriander, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper. Cook for another minute.
Add chicken back to the pan with vegetables. Season with salt and pepper, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until heated through.
To assemble the Moroccan Chili Crisp, place quinoa in a serving dish. Top with the spicy chicken mixture and any desired garnishes like fresh herbs or olives (optional).
Serve warm as part of your brunch spread.
